Customer.title: Customer
Customer.description: Represents a customer who uses the barber shop's services and manages their appointments.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Customer entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the customer.

lastName.description: The last name of the customer.

email.description: The email address of the customer.

phoneNumber.description: The phone number of the customer.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the customer record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the customer record was last updated.

ServiceCategory.title: ServiceCategory
ServiceCategory.description: Groups barber services into categories for better organization and navigation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ServiceCategory entity.

name.description: The name of the service category (e.g., 'Haircuts', 'Shaves').

description.description: A brief description of the service category.

Service.title: Service
Service.description: Defines a specific barber service offered, including its details and pricing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Service entity.

name.description: The name of the service (e.g., 'Gentleman's Haircut', 'Classic Shave').

description.description: A detailed description of what the service includes.

durationMinutes.description: The estimated duration of the service in minutes.

Barber.title: Barber
Barber.description: Represents a barber working at the shop, including their profile and specialties.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Barber entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the barber.

lastName.description: The last name of the barber.

bio.description: A short biography of the barber, highlighting their specialties and style.

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the barber's profile picture.

yearsOfExperience.description: The number of years the barber has been practicing.

BarberSchedule.title: BarberSchedule
BarberSchedule.description: Defines the regular working hours for a specific barber on a given day of the week.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BarberSchedule entity.

dayOfWeek.description: The day of the week this schedule applies to (e.g., 'Monday', 'Tuesday').

startTime.description: The start time of the barber's shift for the day, in HH:MM format.

endTime.description: The end time of the barber's shift for the day, in HH:MM format.

Appointment.title: Appointment
Appointment.description: Represents a confirmed booking for a service with a specific barber at a particular time.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Appointment entity.

appointmentTime.description: The start time of the appointment, in HH:MM format.

status.description: The current status of the appointment (e.g., 'booked', 'confirmed', 'completed', 'cancelled', 'no-show').

notes.description: Any additional notes provided by the customer for the appointment.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the appointment was booked.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the appointment record was last updated.

PortfolioImage.title: PortfolioImage
PortfolioImage.description: An image belonging to a barber's portfolio, showcasing their work.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PortfolioImage entity.

description.description: A description or caption for the image, detailing the style or work shown.

uploadedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the image was uploaded.

GalleryImage.title: GalleryImage
GalleryImage.description: A general image for the salon's gallery, showcasing work or ambiance.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GalleryImage entity.

description.description: A description or caption for the image.

category.description: The category of the gallery image (e.g., 'Haircuts', 'Shaves', 'Ambiance').

uploadedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the image was uploaded.

StyleRecommendation.title: StyleRecommendation
StyleRecommendation.description: A recommended hairstyle or beard style suggested by the AI tool.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StyleRecommendation entity.

name.description: The name of the recommended style (e.g., 'Classic Pompadour', 'Full Beard').

description.description: A detailed description of the style and its characteristics.

imageUrl.description: URL to an image illustrating the recommended style.

faceShapeCompatibility.description: An array of face shapes that are compatible with this style (e.g., ['oval', 'square']).

hairTypeCompatibility.description: An array of hair types that are compatible with this style (e.g., ['straight', 'wavy', 'curly']).

lengthCategory.description: The general length category of the hairstyle (e.g., 'short', 'medium', 'long').

definition.description: Stores individual customer profiles. The `customerId` corresponds to the authenticated user's UID (request.auth.uid), enforcing ownership and privacy. Authorization is path-based, ensuring independence.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the customer, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores definitions of service categories. This collection is publicly readable by all users. Managed by administrators to maintain public catalog integrity.

params.name: serviceCategoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for a service category.

definition.description: Stores details of individual barber services. This collection is publicly readable by all users. Managed by administrators. Includes a `serviceCategoryId` foreign key.

params.name: serviceId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific service.

definition.description: Stores profiles for barbers, including their bio and experience. This collection is publicly readable by all users, allowing clients to view barber information.

params.name: barberId
params.description: The unique identifier for a barber.

definition.description: Stores the regular working hours for a specific barber. This subcollection is publicly readable to facilitate appointment booking. Authorization is implicit through parent path for read access, explicit barberId if write is granted to barber.

params.name: barberId
params.description: The unique identifier of the barber this schedule belongs to.

params.name: scheduleId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific barber's schedule entry.

definition.description: Stores details of customer appointments. Includes denormalized `customerId` and `barberId` fields for authorization independence. Customers can read/manage their own appointments, barbers can read/update their own, and admins can manage all. Supports secure `list` operations (QAPs) via direct field filtering.

params.name: appointmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for an appointment.

definition.description: Stores images showcasing a barber's work. This subcollection is publicly readable, providing visual portfolios for each barber. Authorization for read is implicit through parent path.

params.name: barberId
params.description: The unique identifier of the barber to whom this portfolio image belongs.

params.name: imageId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific portfolio image.

definition.description: Stores general salon gallery images. This collection is publicly readable by all users, showcasing the salon's ambiance and work. Managed by administrators.

params.name: imageId
params.description: The unique identifier for a gallery image.

definition.description: Stores AI-generated style recommendations. This collection is publicly readable by all users accessing the AI tool. Managed by administrators or a dedicated service account.

params.name: recommendationId
params.description: The unique identifier for a style recommendation.

definition.description: Dedicated collection for administrative roles (DBAC). Admin status is determined by the existence of a document where `adminId` matches `request.auth.uid`. Content of the document is minimal, often just a placeholder or role metadata. This enables authorization independence via existence checks.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique identifier of an administrator, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore data structure prioritizes authorization independence, debuggability, and query efficiency, adhering strictly to the provided design principles. User-specific data, such as customer profiles, are stored in path-based collections (`/customers/{customerId}`), where the document ID directly corresponds to the authenticated user's UID (`request.auth.uid`). This ensures that only the customer themselves can access their personal data, making authorization rules simple and explicit.

For entities with public read access, such as `ServiceCategory`, `Service`, `Barber` profiles, `GalleryImage`, and `StyleRecommendation`, data is segregated into top-level collections (e.g., `/services`, `/barbers`). This structural segregation (`B. Structural Segregation`) means all documents within these collections share the same security posture (publicly readable), simplifying rules dramatically. `list` operations on these collections are inherently secure as they are designed for public consumption (`QAPs`).

Nested data for public entities, like a barber's schedule or portfolio images, uses hierarchical paths (`/barbers/{barberId}/schedules/{scheduleId}` and `/barbers/{barberId}/portfolioImages/{imageId}`). While nested, these are still primarily public read collections, meaning authorization doesn't depend on complex `get()` calls to the parent document for basic read access. If write access were to be granted to barbers, the `schedule` or `portfolioImage` document itself would likely include the `barberId` for direct authorization without needing to retrieve the parent `barber` document, upholding `A. Authorization Independence`.

The `Appointment` entity is central and involves multiple parties (customer and barber). To achieve `A. Authorization Independence` and support `QAPs`, `Appointment` documents are stored in a top-level collection (`/appointments/{appointmentId}`). Critically, each `Appointment` document includes both the `customerId` and `barberId` fields. This denormalization allows security rules to directly check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.customerId` or `request.auth.uid == resource.data.barberId` without requiring any `get()` operations. For `list` operations, customers can securely query by `customerId == request.auth.uid`, and barbers by `barberId == request.auth.uid`, directly satisfying `QAPs` as the `where` clauses match the security rule conditions. This approach eliminates the need for complex, transaction-breaking `get()` calls in rules, making them robust and easily debuggable.

Administrative roles are handled using the `C. Global Roles (DBAC)` strategy, with a dedicated collection (`/roles_admin/{adminId}`). Authorization for admin actions relies on the *existence* of a document with the authenticated user's UID in this collection, using `exists()` in security rules. This is an atomic operation and does not break transactions or `A. Authorization Independence`. Data clarity and predictable schema (`D. Data Clarity and Predictability`) are maintained by explicitly naming parameters (e.g., `{customerId}`, `{barberId}`) and using clear entity names derived from the provided schemas.
